notes_md:
**con** is a classifier mainly used for animals in Vietnamese. You put it before the animal noun.


It’s also used for some things that feel “alive,” moving, or long—like rivers, roads, or insects—so don’t be surprised when it’s not just animals.  

Another very common meaning is **“child”** (your son/daughter), and it’s also how kids refer to themselves when talking to their parents.  

Think of **con** as the go-to classifier for living creatures—and sometimes for things Vietnamese people see as having life or movement.

translation.en: I have a child at home.

notes:
con is a classifier mainly used for animals in Vietnamese. You put it before the animal noun.

It’s also used for some things that feel “alive,” moving, or long—like rivers, roads, or insects—so don’t be surprised when it’s not just animals.  

Another very common meaning is “child” (your son/daughter), and it’s also how kids refer to themselves when talking to their parents.  

Think of con as the go-to classifier for living creatures—and sometimes for things Vietnamese people see as having life or movement.
exampleSentences: Nhà anh nuôi một con chó rất dễ thương và hiền lành.
Ở sở thú, em thấy rất nhiều con voi lớn đi quanh sân.
Chị rất thích ngắm những con chim nhỏ bay trên bầu trời sáng sớm.

definitions.es: Un niño o descendencia; también puede referirse a un animal.
definitions.en: A child or offspring; can also refer to an animal.
definitions.vi: Một đứa trẻ hoặc con cái; cũng có thể chỉ một con vật.

example.vi: Con mèo đang ngủ trên ghế.
example.en: The cat is sleeping on the chair.
example.es: El gato está durmiendo en la silla.

exampleSentencesTranslated: My family has a very cute and gentle animal.
At the zoo, I saw many large animal walking around the yard.
She really likes watching small animal fly in the early morning sky.
